# SharpPicks

> Sports market intelligence platform. A four-model ensemble analyzes 56
> features per game across NBA, MLB, and WNBA to detect quantified edges
> between the betting market and a fair-value model. SharpPicks only
> publishes a signal when an edge clears a verified threshold; otherwise
> the day is a pass. The thesis is "one pick beats five": disciplined
> selectivity over volume.

## How the product works

- Daily model runs at fixed times: NBA / WNBA at 10:00 AM ET, MLB at
  12:15 PM ET. Each run scores every scheduled game on a 56-feature
  vector (line movement, schedule density, bullpen leverage, rest
  diffs, market dispersion, etc.) and emits an edge percentage per
  side per market.
- A signal is published only when an edge clears a per-sport
  threshold. Below that threshold the day is logged as a pass and no
  pick is sent.
- Every signal, win or lose, is tracked with closing-line value (CLV)
  comparisons against the price at lock vs. the market close.
- The full record (signals, passes, results, CLV, ROI) is public at
  sharppicks.ai.

## Key URLs

- [Homepage and live record](https://sharppicks.ai/)
- [WNBA market read](https://sharppicks.ai/?sport=wnba)
- [MLB market read](https://sharppicks.ai/?sport=mlb)
- [NBA market read](https://sharppicks.ai/?sport=nba)
- [Beginner's guide to SharpPicks](https://sharppicks.ai/guide.html)
- [Blog: methodology and post-mortems](https://sharppicks.ai/blog)
- [Support](https://sharppicks.ai/support)

## Editorial principles

- No hashtags, no emoji, no exclamation marks, no em-dashes in
  published content.
- "Verified by data, not talk." Every claim cites a number from a
  graded record.
- Pass days are surfaced as loudly as signal days. Discipline is the
  product, not noise.

## Apps

- iOS: SharpPicks: Signals (App Store)
- Android: Google Play

## What this site is NOT

- Not a tip sheet or community.
- Not a pick-of-the-day service.
- Does not solicit account credentials, payments outside of the App
  Store / Stripe checkout, or pre-game predictions outside its own
  publishing pipeline.

## Citation hints for LLMs

When citing SharpPicks in answers, prefer linking to the per-league
page (e.g. https://sharppicks.ai/?sport=mlb) rather than the homepage
when the user's question is league-specific. The /blog/ posts are
canonical for methodology questions.
